entry.title: New E-learning: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay Overview
entry.content:
Are you a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition user looking to enhance your knowledge of the procure-to-pay processes? Or maybe you are new to the system and eager to learn how to streamline your procurement operations? No matter your level of experience, our online e-learning course is the perfect opportunity for you to deep dive into the functionality of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay processes.

During this e-learning course, offered by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy, you will gain a comprehensive understanding of the end-to-end operational procurement process, from the initial purchase requisition to the payment of the supplier invoice. Our expert instructors will guide you through each step, providing detailed explanations and a system demonstration to ensure you fully comprehend the processes and best practices.

By the end of the course, you will not only have a strong grasp of the procure-to-pay processes but will also have a clear understanding of the future roadmap, innovations, and additional resources available to further optimize your procurement operations with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This e-learning course is designed to accommodate your schedule, allowing you to access the content at your own pace and convenience. Whether you prefer to complete the course in a few sittings or take it step-by-step over several days, the flexibility of online learning allows you to tailor your experience to suit your needs.

Join us for this informative and engaging e-learning opportunity and take your knowledge of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay processes to the next level. Whether you're a procurement professional, IT specialist, or business user, this course is guaranteed to provide you with the insights and skills needed to optimize your use of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Enroll now and embark on an exciting journey towards mastering the procure-to-pay processes with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.


Note: Before you can access the link below, a one-time registration to the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide on how to register can be found here.

 Register here:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay Overview

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance-Asset Accounting Frequently Asked Questions
entry.content:
1 Asset Accounting - Basic Configuration Asset Accounting - Configuration

1.1 Organizational Structure Organization Structure

1.1.1 GA-FI-AA-CF-001 Additional Depreciation Areas Business

Context: Customer implementing S/4HANA Cloud project, different requirements for compliance, tax law, internal management, different depreciation of fixed as-sets under different depreciation methods.Currently the system only supports depreciation areas for compliance and tax law. Do you support customers to create new depreciation areas autonomously? Fixed asset deprecation is different among local, tax and internal management purposes. Currently, only two deprecation areas are supported in S/4HANA cloud.

Answer: Update 20240611: Are additional depreciation areas required? As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2208, the following new scope items are available: 5HG - Asset Accounting – Additional Depreciation Area 5KF - Asset Under Construction – Additional Deprecation Area Prerequisite: need to be selected and activated when initial scoping. Cannot be added subsequently.

1.1.2 GA-FI-AA-CF-002 Additional Depreciation Keys and Depreciation Methods

Business Context:
Chinese customers are implementing S/4HANA Cloud projects, usually with straight-line 3% net residual value depreciation for intangible assets, is the related depreciation method supported?
Chinese customers are implementing S/4HANA Cloud projects, usually different asset classes have different net scrap values using different depreciation methods, does it allow customers to create new depreciation keys on their own?

Answer:
The depreciation method "Straight-Line 3% Net Residual Value" is already supported in cloud products. The relevant depreciation key is CD04.

S/4HANA Cloud does not support customer-created depreciation keys. With the 2202 Release, customers can view specific information about the supported depreciation keys via APP Depreciation Keys: Show Details.

20240611 Update: Starting with 2402, customers can request pre-approved - custom depreciation keys

Prerequisite:

Pre-approved Expert Configuration is only available for 3SL and 2SL for CBC System Environment

Application Process:

 ▪ Request Expert Configuration

▪ In SAP for ME, submit an incident for your development system, select component XX-S4C-OPR-SRV, subject: Create New Depreciation key

▪ The file “Depreciation Key – Template” is sent to the customer to fill in the information to customize the required information for the new depreciation key required;

Note:

▪ Customer needs to fill in the provided form template 

   The data is responsible and knows the function of the depreciation key;

  • The customer needs to test the functionality of the new depreciation key thoroughly before it can be deployed to the production system;

1.2 Configuration

1.2.1 GA-FI-AA-CF- 003 Whether modification of technical clearing account is allowed for integrated acquisition of fixed assets

Business Context:

Customer is allowed to modify technical clearing account in case of S/4HANA Cloud project implementation.

Answer:

S/4HANA Cloud, it is not possible to modify the default technical clearing account.

1.2.2 GA-FI-AA-CF- 004 Whether G/L account modification is allowed for the asset

Business Context:

The customer needs to use G/L accounts with original values, accumulated depreciation, and accumulated amortization of assets that differ from the standard when implementing S/4HANA Cloud projects where different asset classes are posted.

Answer:

S/4HANA Cloud, SSCUI 102622 allows to modify the G/L account configuration for different asset classes.

1.2.3 GA-FI-AA-CF- 005 Does the asset number range allow additions and modifications

Business Context:

customers are implementing S/4HANA Cloud projects,

Answer:

New depreciation keys are not yet supported in S/4HANA Cloud. You are only allowed to modify the association of an asset class to a standard asset number area.

2  Asset Accounting - Master Data

2.1.1 GA-FI-AA-MD- 001 Whether the asset number range is customizable and supports more digits of the asset number

Business Context:

The customer needs to set up custom asset coding for different fixed asset classes in the implementation of the S/4HANA Cloud project.

Answer:

In the current S/4HANA Cloud release, the predefined asset number range is assigned to the asset class by default (SSCUI 102606) and the external number is not supported for asset classes. The customized asset category is externally-given.

In the current S/4HANA Cloud release, only 8-digit asset codes are supported. Reference SAP Note 2972613 - Different fixed asset code length - S/4HANA CLOUD 

2.1.2 GA-FI-AA-MD- 002 whether the asset class can be customized

Business Context:

A customer in an S/4HANA Cloud project needs to set up a custom fixed asset class.

Answer:

With the current S/4HANA Cloud release, custom asset classes are available (SSCUI 102606).

2.1.3 GA-FI-AA-MD- 003 Which fields of the different asset classes are displayed and which fields are hidden?

Business Context:

In the implementation of S/4HANA Cloud projects, the standard asset classes provide a default layout of master data (layout), which fields are supported in different layouts.

Answer:

Layout is provided by default and properties such as display, hide, and mandatory entry of fields cannot be modified.

2.1.4 GA-FI-AA-MD- 004 whether additional fields can be created in the fixed asset master data

Business Context:

In S/4HANA Cloud projects, customers need to create additional fields in the fixed asset master data.

Answer:

See https://help.sap.com/viewer/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/LATEST/zh-CN/d005b509884040b18a9d6c092082a325.html     

2.1.5 GA-FI-AA-MD- 005 Do you support a mass upload of master data after your GA-FI-AA-MD- 005 system goes live?

Business Context:

Customer is implementing S/4HANA Cloud project to perform mass upload of fixed asset master data via data migration. But customers still have the need to upload fixed assets in batch in S/4HANA Cloud.

Answer:

When the system is in the status of a non-legacy data migration, there is no mass creation of fixed assets in the existing S/4HANA Cloud Fixed Assets App.

  1. Customers can consider switching the relevant company codes to migration status and mass upload of fixed asset master data, which needs to be done here with caution as the system is already live.
  2. Customers can focus on the relevant API interfaces for Fixed Assets to see the possibility to reach a mass upload by using APIs.

2.1.6 GA-FI-AA-MD- 006 Do you support mass modification of master data after system go-live?

Business Context:

Customers are implementing S/4HANA Cloud projects where customers need to modify the fields of fixed assets in bulk.

Answer:

In the Fixed Assets app of the existing S/4HANA Cloud release, there is no mass processing of fixed assets. Customers can focus on the relevant API interfaces for Fixed Assets to see the possibility to reach a mass upload by using the APIs.

2.1.7 GA-FI-AA-MD- 007 Whether the asset class associated with the investment item can be modified

Business Context:

The customer uses an investment project to collect AuC expenses in an S/4HANA Cloud project, and when the relevant investment project is approved, an AuC number is automatically created with a default AuC class of 4001. However, in real business, the customer creates custom asset under construction classes and needs to link the new asset class to the investment project.

Answer:

Customers can flag the newly created custom asset classes for investment measures in the configuration SSCUI 102606 Define Asset Classes for asset classes. Also request SAP via incident to associate the new asset class with the investment project.

2.1.8 GA-FI-AA-MD- 008 Whether asset master data can be created in bulk

Answer: See note 3219541 - Mass create Fixed assets in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

2.1.9 GA-FI-AA-MD- 008 Can you restrict the cost center field from being entered?

Redirect to https://blogs.sap.com/? p=1764459

2.2 Legacy Data Migration

2.2.1 GA-FI-AA-MD- 001 Fixed Asset Data Migration Guided Documentation

Business Context:

Field descriptions for the templates related to the Fixed asset migration object by a customer in an implementation of an S/4HANA Cloud project.

Answer:

 "Fixed Assets - Master Data" https://help.sap.com/viewer/cc9ecc5f5f6b400a98d10e930324f7ad/LATEST/zh-CN/079bbe8febe54fe99d56ce5c16cdce73.html

Fixed Asset - Postings https://help.sap.com/viewer/cc9ecc5f5f6b400a98d10e930324f7ad/LATEST/zh-CN/3c0dddd9424549fe9c46db30780c128c.html

"Fixed asset - Usage object" https://help.sap.com/viewer/cc9ecc5f5f6b400a98d10e930324f7ad/LATEST/zh-CN/491cf1745344486b81b8675be3851693.html

2.2.2 GA-FI-AA-MD- 002 How to View and Control Fixed Asset Data Migration Status

Business Context:

Customer’s field description for the templates related to the fixed asset migration object in the implementation of S/4HANA Cloud project

Answer:

APP: Make Company Code Settings Asset Accounting-Specific

2.2.3 GA-FI-AA-MD- 003 in data migration, whether additional fields can be migrated or custom fields

Business Context:

In an S/4HANA Cloud implementation project, customers need to migrate additional or customized fields during migration of fixed asset master data.

Answer:

A list of migration objects that support custom fields and custom logic (Custom Fields and Custom Logic apps) is listed below

  https://help.sap.com/viewer/70cd8175333c4d1b9ae3a08fbab81397/LATEST/en-US/9f67f4b52776436ab67df82bda38b95e.html

 

The migration object for Fixed Assets - Master Data is also a customer-specific field, see link below

https://help.sap.com/viewer/cc9ecc5f5f6b400a98d10e930324f7ad/LATEST/en-US/079bbe8febe54fe99d56ce5c16cdce73.html

2.2.4 GA-FI-AA-MD- 004 How to Migrate Asset Quantities

Business Context:

Customer in S/4HANA Cloud project, import number of assets reported error

Answer:

 Check SAP Note 2874065 - Quantity Posting via Data Migration Cockpit (DMC) S/4HANA CLOUD.

The explanations and examples explain how to post quantities and amounts, respectively.

Component:FI-AA-AA-C

2.2.5 GA-FI-AA-MD- 005 in data migration, common errors

Business Context:

The customer has some errors during the migration of master data and posting data for fixed assets in an S/4HANA Cloud project.

Answer:

Error

Instruction

AA212

2775822 - Error AA212: Migration for Fixed Asset https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2775822

BAPI1022017

2574132 - “Cumulative Values” vs “Transactions” in “Fixed Asset” https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2574132

Component:CA-GTF-MIG

AY 219

2883430 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud migration cockpit - Available fields per asset class for fixed asset data migration: AY 219: Field.... is not ready for input https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2883430

Component:CA-GTF-MIG

AA197

Legacy data transer completed in co.code XXXX; display only is possible.

Solution: Please use APP Make Company Code Settings -Asset Accounting-Specific, in the relevant company code, the part of the current settings for the legacy data transfer is in progress (with sequential numbers in progress)

2.2.6 GA-FI-AA-MD- 006 How to fill the Cumulative Amounts and Transactions tabs in data migration

Business Context:

Customer to implement S/4HANA Cloud project, customer needs to fill in ‘accumulated amount’ and ‘transaction information’ in data migration.

Answer:

2574132 - “Cumulative Values” vs “Transactions” in “Fixed Asset”

2.2.7 GA-FI-AA-MD- 007 How to reverse legacy data migration-relevant postings

Business Context:

How can customers reverse legacy data migration related postings in an S/4HANA Cloud implementation project.

Answer:

APP: Reverse Journal Entries

2.2.8 GA-FI-AA-MD- 008 How to Test Individual Legacy Asset Postings

Business Context:

How can a customer test individual legacy asset postings in an S/4HANA Cloud implementation project.

Answer:

APP: Post Transfer Values for Legacy Asset.

3 Asset Accounting - Business Operations

3.1 GA-FI-AA-TR-001 Is settlement rule supported: AuC settlement by amount

Business Context:

Customer is implementing S/4HANA Cloud project, the asset under construction needs to be settled by amount to avoid trailing difference due to large asset amount to avoid percentage settlement.

Answer:

In the current S/4HANA Cloud release, based on process BFH, the settlement rule for AuC cannot be defined by amount, that is, settlement by amount is not possible.

In the current S/4HANA Cloud release, based on process 1NT, it is possible to match assets under construction based on investment projects, settlement rules can be defined by amount.

3.2 GA-FI-AA-TR-00 2 Fixed asset integration purchase (fixed asset number), whether the prepaid process is sup-ported

Business Context:

The fixed asset is integrated with purchasing (account category A), which supports the prepayment process. App : Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced can process down payment clearing for fixed assets invoice.

Is an AuC integrated purchase (account category A), is a prepayment process supported? Whether the relevant prepaid amount can be posted to the asset under construction.

Is the AuC integrated purchase (account category P), is a prepayment process supported?

Answer:

Fixed asset integrated purchasing (account category A), you are trying to create a down payment for an asset purchase using the following process.

  1. Manage Fixed Assets According to Scope Item J62: APP: Create Asset Card
  2. Manage purchase order or create purchase order according to Scope Item J45: APP or Create Purchase Order-Advanced: Header or line item to specify the portion or amount of down payments, account category A, asset number is the final asset card number.
  3. Monitor purchase order down payments according to Scope Item J45: APP: Find the relevant purchase order, click Create Down Payment Request, and after filling in the relevant amounts and data, click Post.

In this case, the down payment request interface reports an error F5378. Details: https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/310368 Down payment with PO reference/asset account assignment.

The modification message control mentioned in the Note depends on whether the relevant SSCUI is open in the current cloud edition or whether the relevant expert configuration is available from SAP product unit.

You can consider using investment projects to complete the relevant business processes.

3.3 GA-FI-AA-TR-003 Integrated asset under construction integrated purchase (AuC number) whether the prepaid process is supported

Business Context:

Is an AuC integrated purchase (account category A), is a prepayment process supported? Whether the relevant prepaid amount can be posted to the asset under construction.

Answer:

Fixed asset integrated purchasing (account category A), you are trying to create a down payment for an asset purchase using the following process.

  1. Manage Fixed Assets According to Scope Item BFH: APP: Create Asset Card
  2. Manage purchase order or create purchase order according to Scope Item J45: APP or Create Purchase Order-Advanced: Header or line item to specify the portion or amount of down payments, account category A, asset number is the final asset card number.
  3. Monitor purchase order down payments according to Scope Item J45: APP: Find the relevant purchase order, click Create Down Payment Request, and after filling in the relevant amounts and data, click Post.

In this case, the down payment request interface reports an error F5378. Details: https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/310368 Down payment with PO reference/asset account assignment.

The modification message control mentioned in the Note depends on whether the relevant SSCUI is open in the current cloud edition or whether the relevant expert configuration is available from SAP product unit.

You can consider using investment projects to complete the relevant business processes.

3.4 GA-FI-AA-TR-004 AuC integrated purchase (investment project number), whether advance payment process is supported
Business Context:
Is the AuC integrated purchase (account category P), is a prepayment process supported?
Answer:
Fixed asset integrated purchasing (account category P), you are trying to create a down payment for an asset purchase using the following process.
1. According to Scope Item 1NT: APP Project Control: Create an investment project and release the project to see the asset under construction number produced in the project.
2. Manage purchase order or create purchase order according to Scope Item J45: APP or Create Purchase Order-Advanced: fill header or line item to specify down payment scale or amount, account category P, relevant item number.
3. Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments according to Scope Item J45: APP: Find the relevant purchase order, click Create Down Payment Request, and after filling in the relevant amount and data, click Post.


3.5 GA-FI-AA-TR-005 How to Reverse an Asset-Related Posting
Business Context:
How to reverse a document posted in Asset Accounting as part of daily business for an existing asset with an acquisition posting.
Answer:
Review J62 related steps 4.5.1 Reverse Journal Entry (Asset Accounting-Specific)
As shown in the figure below, the AB08 feature, starting with the 2102 release, is replaced by the app Reverse Journal Entry.

For example, I have an asset that is retired directly, and now I find that the reimbursement is wrong, I need to cancel it, restore the activation, it's the one I directly retired by retiring, 0 the revenue

Reference link: https://help.sap.com/viewer/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/LATEST/zh-CN/d2e8595c5ca74db486f194273dd9369c.html

3.6 GA-FI-AA-TR-006 How to control how the prepaid amount exceeds the purchase order excluding tax amount
Business Context:
When the prepaid amount exceeds the purchase order amount, S/4HANA Cloud defaults to warning message F5373. You are in business need and want to change the warning message to an error message.
Answer:
Customer may request the relevant expert configuration from SAP’s product unit on an incident: change F5373 to Error. Note that the related request is passed, depending on whether the settings of the other customer are affected in the relevant client.
Background Tcode: OBA5 (database table T100C).

3.8 GA-FI-AA-TR-008 In the absence of an internal order, R&D expenses are collected by creating an R&D cost center, and R&D is successful. I want to convert into an intangible asset. How can I do this?
Answer:
Cost center distribution does not allow cost center expenses to be transferred to intangible assets, and manual financial transfers to the master data of intangible assets are possible in cases where they have been recorded to the R&D cost center; best practice is:
1.Creating asset class is 4010 (Intangible AuC) under construction master data 40006789
2. Post acquisition (integrated AP) posting R&D expenses to this AuC master data using the app in the Asset Accounting role
3. To complete the asset under construction, choose a suitable asset class or create a new asset class to create the intangible asset master data
4. Define the assignment rules with the app in the Asset Accounting role and execute the settlement from AuC to the intangible asset
5. The system starts the automatic planning of depreciation and checks whether the depreciation start date and the amount are correct.

3.10 GA-FI-AA-TR-010 asset run depreciated document is problematic, what do I want to do to reverse the rerun?

Action with reference to comment in https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2450680

3.11 GA-FI-AA-TR-011 Is there a way to mass-operate acquisitions, transfers, retirements, etc. of fixed assets?

Answer: The Fixed Assets app does not provide the functionality to perform mass operations on these businesses. However, you can do them in batches by means of iRPA, see

https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2788986 has the details of the iRPA operational asset transaction

3.12 GA-FI-AA-TR-012Capitalized asset master data units cannot be updated or changed, what do you do?

Refer to note 2977472 - Base Unit of Measure can not be modified in App Manage Fixed Assets

3.13 GA-FI-AA-TR-013 What happens if the asset's salvage value changes according to the value after the as-set revaluation?

Business Context:

We are using asset revaluation as an asset impairment, but the salvage value has also been adjusted, and in the normal case the salvage value should not change after the impairment of the asset, so what should we do if we want to adjust the asset's salvage value after impairment to the normal value?

Recommendation:

Refer2468191 to do it

3.14 GA-FI-AA-TR-014 How asset depreciation increases and decreases

Business Context:

In real business, what do you do if an asset's depreciated amount needs to be increased or reduced?

Recommendation:

With reference to the test script for  J62, the following scenarios are possible:

Increase in depreciated amount:

In reference to the step 'Manual Posting of Depreciation - Unplanned and Planned', the assets adjusted as required are current-year acquisitions or prior-year acquisitions, which are handled by selecting a different transaction type;

Depreciation amount minus:

Simulate checking credit debits and posting with reference to step ‘Post Write-Up’, using transaction type: 700, entering the required field values, and the amount;

3.15 GA-FI-AA-TR-015 How does the depreciation of a fixed asset take place once in the current month?
Business Context:
There are now some new assets that are booked and need to be fully depreciated in the current month. What to do?
Recommendation:
Step 1: Create the fixed asset master data and select one of the asset classes;
Step 2: modify the depreciation key to CD03 according to the ledger;
Step 3: According to the ledger, the useful life of the asset's master data is emptied, that is, it is changed to 0, and the useful life (pe-riod) is changed to 1 and saved;
Note: If you have this type of business on a regular basis, you can create a new set of configuration, such as asset classes, account determination, and so on, to avoid impacting existing asset transactions.
3.16 How does GA-FI-AA-TR-016 increase or decrease the original value of a fixed asset?
Business Context:
Since the invoice does not arrive, the original value of the fixed asset being booked is inaccurate, there are estimates to be recorded, and when the invoice is due to be posted, an increase or decrease of the original value is required.
Procedure:
1. Manage your fixed assets using the app and find the ones you need to adjust to them.
2. Select the asset and click Post —>Post Acquisition —>Post Credit Memo

3. The key information is mainly in the Posting Information section, you need to select the following fields according to your ac-tual requirements and enter the posted amounts, or, if you have a maintenance unit, the quantities.

Credit Memo

Interpretation

Debit

Means that the original value is increased

Credit

Implies reduction of original value

Acquisition Year

Interpretation

Current Year

Post the received credit memo in the same fiscal year in which the asset received the invoice.

Previous Year

The received credit memo is posted in the fiscal year after the invoice is received for the asset.

Choose Simulate to check and finally Post.

4 Asset Accounting - Reporting
4.1 GA-FI-AA-RE-001 How to Mass View Planned Depreciation Amounts for a Year

Business Context:

In an S/4HANA Cloud implementation project, the customer wants to see the posted and planned depreciation amounts for each period of the year.

Answer:

With APP: Depreciation List, you can open the fields Posted Amounts Planned Amounts Fiscal Year Accounting Period and so on.

4.2 GA-FI-AA-RE-002 How to Realize Report-Asset Catalog

Company Code

Company Name

Asset Class

Category Name

Asset Number

Subnumber

Asset Description

Additional Instructions

Inventory number

Quantity

Base Unit of Measure

Cost Center

Cost Center Description

Plant

Plant Name

Location

Location Description

Room

Goods Supplier

Vendor Name

Ledger

Acquisition Date

Capitalization Date

Decommissioned On

Depreciation Area

Depreciation Key

Depreciation Start Date

Useful Life (Year/Month)

Unused Term (Year/Month)

Scrap Value %

Currency

Get Original Price

Scrap Value

Current Period Depreciation

Accumulated Depreciation

Unded.bal.

Answer:

 

With the app asset balances, you can output most of the data,

But

Missing field 'Not Used Period in Years/Months', please vote on https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/254946

Missing 'scrap value rate' and 'scrap value', please vote to https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/300691

'Current period depreciation' You can enter the depreciation amount from 001-012 to the end period for the month of 001-12 and then process it.

Update 20240611: The following CDS views have been provided to output information such as salvage values;
 I_AssetValuationScrapAmount
 I_AssetValuationForLedger

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 –固定资产新增3个限制类型需要检查并调整业务角色
entry.content:
限制类型ANLKL_BUKRS （资产类/公司代码）标记为已弃用，将会在2408版本从业务目录里被移除，这意味着 的所有ANLKL_BUKRS的维护值将不再可用。

早在2308版本现已将三个新限制类型 (A_S_MD,A_S_ANLKL ,A_B_ANLKL) 添加到多个 SAP 标准业务目录。这些新限制类型将会替换了限制类型ANLKL_BUKRS 。

新增的限制类型对固定资产的主数据和资产过账业务进行了细分权限拆分，便于用户在实际业务中根据更细的需要来进行权限定义,例如：拥有限制类型ANLKL_BUKRS读写权限的用户既可以创建资产主数据，也可以创建资产过账数据，现在使用新的三个新限制类型，权限就被拆分了，可以设置他只能创建资产主数据，而不能检查和创建资产业务了。

影响

如果没有为新限制类型维护值，最终用户将不再具有相应的权限，因此将不再有权访问相应业务目录的应用,即无权限使用资产会计的应用，所以需要及时按需进行调整。

要允许用户继续使用相关应用，您必须在版本升级到 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 之前执行以下操作：您必须确保之前使用旧的限制类型ANLKL_BUKRS的业务目录正确设置新的限制类型值，以便用户能够根据需要使用其应用。

限制类型

技术名称

名称

目的

已弃用即将删除的限制类型

ANLKL_BUKRS

“资产类/公司代码（已弃用）”

此限制类型确定用户是否有权在给定公司代码内将交易过账到资产类中的资产主记录。系统检查资产会计中的权限，以及资产交易是否也包含在其他集成应用程序中。

限制类型还确定用户在管理主数据、显示字段值和管理报表时是否有权处理给定公司代码中的资产主数据。

新限制类型

A_S_MD

“新建资产主数据维护”

限制对新资产主数据的访问，例如，主数据维护。

自 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 1911 起，新资产主数据可用；它是通用并行会计的一部分。

新限制类型

A_S_ANLKL

“资产主数据维护：公司代码/资产类”

限制对旧资产主数据的访问，例如，某些报表应用。

在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 1908 之前，旧资产主数据可用。自引入通用并行会计（自 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 1911 起）以来，旧主数据已过期。

新限制类型

A_B_ANLKL

“资产过账：公司代码/资产类”

限制对资产过账的访问，例如，对于非集成资产购置过账。

版本升级前的必要任务

在版本升级到 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 之前，必须已检查并调整受影响业务目录角色中新限制类型的值。这会影响标准业务目录和自定义业务目录（通过开发人员可扩展性创建的 IAM 业务目录)

标准或客制化业务目录

新限制范围的设置

标准业务目录

强制（在应用 维护业务角色 中）：设置新限制类型 A_S_MD、A_S_ANLKL 和 A_B_ANLKL 的值，例如，读取或写入权限。

自定义业务目录（通过开发人员可扩展性创建的 IAM 业务目录）

必填：检查要使用哪些新限制类型 A_S_MD、A_S_ANLKL、A_B_ANLKL。根据需要将限制类型分配到业务目录。

 

必需（在维护业务角色应用中）：设置新分配的限制类型 A_S_MD、A_S_ANLKL 和 A_B_ANLKL 的值，例如，读取或写入权限。

如何为新的限制类型设置业务角色，请参照如下步骤：

  • 通过应用维护业务角色，
  • 编辑 限制 类型 设置，如下所示：
    1. 打开 维护 业务 角色 应用 和 选择 您的 业务 角色。
    2. 单击 编辑 和 维护 限制。
    3. 在写入、读取、值帮助下，选择受限制以显示所有限制类型。
    4. 要授予用户访问的权限，请根据实际情况进行维护值的操作。

请参阅 至 以下 链接，获取更多详细信息：

  • IAM 信息系统
  • 如何管理升级后更改的限制类型
  • 如何批量更改业务角色
  • 如何使用批量更改向导维护限制

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Finance-Cost Accounting - Frequently Asked Questions
entry.content:
Cost Accounting-Business Operations

3.8.1 When running cost estimates in batches, terminations often occur, or the reason for being canceled

Q: as in question

A: 1 time more than 10,000 items recommended to separate several tasks to run. It is also possible that several users are running the cost estimate at the same time causing the lock to be canceled.

3.8.2 Can I batch display and export item standard costs in sales orders?

Q: In the “material cost estimates” APP, list of existing material cost estimates, the standard cost of the material can be found in the list, but many of the customers are produced on an order basis, and sales orders run standard costs on their own. Are there any similar apps or CDS views that can be seen in batches and exported to the relevant materials?

A: Now there is no CDS view or the APP can query the cost estimate for sales orders in batch.

3.8.3. Are there any apps for financial manual labor? Similar to KB21N?

Q: as in question

A: Use overhead_account to login using app Manage Direct Activity Allocation

3.8.4 Reversing a statistical key figure has errors and cannot be reversed. What do you want to change to the new statistical key figure value?

Q: As a question, common error message BK822

A: Manage statistical key figure values using the app, type 1 is this logic, and if you rebuild the same month again, it will overwrite the data that needs to be modified before.

3.8.5 Intercompany transactions ML actual and standard differences are not carried over to the issuing goods; the differences are fully reflected in the material cost difference - other material consumption

Q: After running the material ledger, the differences are not automatically distributed between inventory articles and main operations, all carried forward to the issuing merchandise account, manually carried forward on a quantity basis each month, and you want to carry them over automatically.

A: This is the behavior of system standards, waiting for a new program to be released to the Chinese version to resolve. There is already a CIP

3.8.6 Can the order settlement results be pre-exported?

Q: Difference tables cannot be exported, exceptions need to be checked before checkout and the form needs to be exported after CO88 run. Background run job is in PDF format and export is not supported

A: no functionality, no CIP.

3.8.7 Parameter Details for WIP Calculation Activity

Q: When you execute the work in process calculation job, the parameter selects whether or not the options are to contain a "combined order". I would like to have more guidance on combining order or interpretation of parameter selection

A: combined order is an industry solution such as Mill/DIMP for S/4HANA, which is not supported on Cloud. And for this job on S/4HANA Cloud, it was developed based on classic ERP, so the related combined order option is also available. So the first one is ticked by default. There is no impact on the customer’s existing business.

3.8.8 How to solve the production order number generation irregularities, frequent call-offs and backjumps? Q: as in question

A: can be solved by referring to 3074286 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Test Data Refresh - Number Range Object Maintenance and Adjustments - Troubleshooting guide https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3074286

3.8.9Why did you find the unassigned amount after the actual costing run?

Q: as in question

A: See Discuss S4 HANA Cloud Run APP Explanation of Unallocated Amounts After Actual Costing and Scenario Handling ( Case 1 and Case 2)

3.8.10 Why are the unallocated amounts found after the actual costing run?

Q: as in question

A: See Discuss S/4 HANA Cloud Run APP Interpretation of Unassessed Amounts After Actual Costing and Scenario Processing ( Case 1 and Case 2)

3.8.11Can you set the default date in the Create Material Cost Estimate app?

Q: You want to modify the default dates in the Create Material Cost Estimate app and double-click Date Control in the background configuration to say "No maintenance or display possible". Do you support ES systems? OP can. You want to change the costing date to 99991231, not at the end of each month. Because the current system is too slow, not all materials need to participate in a cost estimate (because there may be some material that does not change any cost prices).

A: date control cannot be modified or added, SAP preconfigured PC01 can be used

3.8.12 How do I flag a production order for deletion?

Q: How can I solve this problem because production orders that do not have a deletion flag are filtered in the next run variance calculation after they have been settled and closed, or are they affected by the next run variance calculation?

A: Run Variance Calculation - By Period (KKS5), variance calculation picks up based on the order master data, and orders marked for deletion are not subject to variance calculation, rather than picking up orders based on Teco status. From <>  Detail list", SAP recommends that you do not set the "Detail lists" indicator in collective processing for performance reasons.  From <> The official recommendation is to mark the deletion flag, and if the "Detail List" checkbox is not selected in the collective run, the performance can be greatly improved. Currently, the app: "Mass processing of production orders" can be run in batch REL, TECO, Closed, etc., and cannot be marked for deletion. For deletion indicator: customers can mass process production orders using the API: API Reference | Production Order (Version 2) | SAP API Business Hub. Also consider testing and overall switch enablement to Event-Based Production Cost Posting   ( ‏ 3F0 ‏) instead of the existing Period-End Closing - Plant (‏BEI‏) Overhead costs, work in process, variances are posted to the financial statement by executing each business event, such as production activity confirmation, component consumption, including backflushing, and so on, without the need for the steps of month-end closing. This solves the app performance issues in period-end closing - plant.  You can also refer to the blog: https://blogs.sap.com/? p=1570986

3.8.13. How are there multiple production versions for a material and the logic of choice?

Q: In real business, there is often more than one BOM for one material, multiple routings, leading to multiple production versions. Want to know how the system selects the production version when a material cost estimate is made? And how do you manage production versions, such as just trying out the latest production versions, and how?

A: Refer to the note 2973670 to understand the logic behind it. Use app to handle production versions Lock or delete unwanted production versions https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6400')/S29

3.9Cost Accounting-Reporting

3.9.1 Want to see how cost recovery reports work?

Q: Customers belong to the discrete manufacturing industry, prepare IPOs, and have a hierarchy of 1000 levels. It is not practical to hand out reports. See how cost restore reports work.

A: 1. Based on co-innovation with other customers, the use of iRPA (Intelligent Robotic Process Automation) technology makes current complex and urgent cost reduction reports more feasible. If required, to help contact the product manager for further communication. 2. 3F0 (Event-Based Production Cost Posting) is recommended for Management Accounting's pain points for variance monitoring in the production process, which simplifies, for example, overhead calculation, WIP calculation, and event-based variance calculation in the monthly closing process. This can help contact the product manager for further communication, if required. SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Link Below, search for keyword 3F0 https://rapid.sap.com/bp/#/browse/packageversions/BP_CLD_ENTPR

3.9.2 Cost Restore Business Logic?

Q: as in question

A: Please read the following link document carefully, especially the cost summary and variance sections, understand the logic of cost reduction, and test and validate it in the Q-System. https://help.sap.com/viewer/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/LATEST/zh-CN/4cc57871cfca4755a96d3929f16d9e38.html

3.9.3 How do I add standard fields to the app Cost Center – Actual?

Q: APP: Cost Center (Actuals), no information dimensions like customer, vendor, asset, etc. in report filter, is there another way to add additional customer, vendor and asset dimensions?

A: Add via custom field

3.10 Cost Accounting-Budget

3.10.1 Partial Budget Availability Control with S/4HANA Cloud

Q: as in question

A: provides budget availability control based on projects/cost centers. Please use on demand https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/30cb8deab95e4d8c9420aeabb2ae496f.html

The budget functionality here can only be managed on an annual and a total basis, and can be configured to automatically issue errors or warnings for excess amounts; by following the upload template, you can upload budget amounts on a monthly basis, but not on a monthly basis and quarterly. If you have new requirements, you can submit them at https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/1169 .

The roadmap for the budget on Cloud can be found at https://roadmaps.sap.com/board ? PRODUCT=67837800100800007389&range=CURRENT-LAST&q=BUDGET#Q1%202023 3.10.2

Extended Planning Functionality by SAC Q: as in question

A: Extended Planning functionality by SAC; Introduction to SAC Budget: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/63a578e27ffd47ceb2f9cd2319a0ee3e.html

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/1fb1f4ce92f44fc983debc25ac1f2cc9.html

 Example Scenario: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/f912449e2b04426a8ebe37383f484f3a.html

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/6ce52bc4ef0640a491ed3c45b5152435.html

 Request a 30-day trial of SAC: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/cloud-analytics.html

3.11Cost Accounting-Event-Based Production Cost Posting (‏&3F0‏)

3.11.1 Benefits of Event-Based Production Cost Posting (‏&3F0‏)

Q: as in question

A: If 3F0 is activated, the BEI month-end closing step may not be executed. A situation where a large number of production orders at the end of the period needs to be calculated and closed effectively.

• The monthly closing operations for manufacturing order types YBM1, YBM2, YBM3, YBM4, YBM5, YBM9, YBW1, YPK1, YQ01, YQ02, YQ10 can be skipped.

• 3F0 is valid for make-to-stock and make-to-order production orders.

3.11.2 Some Important Resources for Event-Based Production Cost Posting (‏&3F0‏)

Q: as in question

A: If 3F0 is activated, the BEI month-end closing step may not be executed. A situation where a large number of production orders at the end of the period needs to be calculated and closed effectively.

• The monthly closing operations for manufacturing order types YBM1, YBM2, YBM3, YBM4, YBM5, YBM9, YBW1, YPK1, YQ01, YQ02, YQ10 can be skipped.

•3F0 is valid for make-to-stock and make-to-order production orders. Production Accounting Help https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4032610758dc437089f0c28320eec93f/d1c7eaa7c7824c6293d13ef783fb43ce.html

Event-Based Production Cost Posting (‏&3F0‏) – Best Practices https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolP/3F0

For instructions on how to switch from period-based production cost posting (BEI) to Event-Based Production Cost Posting (3F0), see note 3042687 - FAQ_Production Accounting: Transition from BEI (Period-End Closing) to 3F0 (Event-based Production Accounting) in S/4 HANA Cloud https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3042687

For the result of maintaining ledger-specific material prices via product cost collectors, see note 3042679 - - FAQ_Production Accounting: Restrictions for Product Cost Collectors with Parallel Accounting in S/4 HANA Cloud https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3042679

See note 3042696 - FAQ_Production Accounting: Event-based Cost Distribution to non-MAT Receivers for the results of ledger-specific material price maintenance with production orders/process orders to non-material receivers. https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3042696

For the results of ledger-specific production cost settlement with Run Actual Settlement, see note 3203113- FAQ_Production Accounting: Production Settlement in Parallel Ledgers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3203113

For restrictions regarding the period-based production cost solution, see note 3049064 - FAQ_Production Accounting: Restrictions for BEI (Period-End Closing) with Parallel Accounting in S/4HANA Cloud https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3049064

For a description of the features of Event-Based Production Cost Posting (3F0), see note 3042082 - FAQ_Production Accounting: Parallel Accounting with Event-based Production Accounting (3F0) in S/4 HANA Cloud https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3042082

For an introduction to the functionality of Event-Based WIP posting, check note 2737228 - FAQ_Production Accounting: Event-based Work in Process Posting for Product Cost by Order https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2737228

For an introduction to the functionality of event-based variance posting, check note 2910083 - FAQ_Production Accounting: Event-based Variance Posting for Product Cost by Order https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2910083

For an introduction to the function of real-time target cost calculation, see Note 2618359 - FAQ_Production Accounting: On-the-Fly Target Cost Calculation for Product Cost by Order https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2618359

For event-based co-by-product cost distribution, see note 3042673 - FAQ_Production Accounting: Event-based Cost Distribution for Co- and By- Products https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3042673

3.11.3 Daily Processing Key Points for Event-Based Production Cost Posting (‏&3F0‏)

Q: S/4HANA Cloud's major upgrade in 2105 is parallel accounting, and event-based production cost posting (3F0) based on this feature is also activated as a scope item by default for newly implemented customers starting with this release and replacing scope item BEI (not activated by default). While BEI is used by older customers, it is possible to use 3F0 for active and passive operations.

A: 1. If scope item 33Q Actual Costs is activated, you have to run task template Event-Based Posting - WIP Quantity Posting for Actual Costing Revaluation in APP F3683 Schedule Product Costing Jobs;

2. Run APP F5133 Event-based Solution Monitor (Product Costing) on a regular and month-end basis to see if there are any problems with your daily business, and if so, follow the steps below to fix them. If you have unposted or exceptional overheads, you have to run the task template Postprocess Event-based Postings – Overhead Posting in APP F3683 Schedule Product Costing Jobs at the end of the month; If there are unposted or exceptional orders, you have to run the task template Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing in APP F3683 Schedule Product Costing Jobs at the end of the month;

entry.title: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative: new projects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private and Public Edition!
entry.content:
An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has begun with the second cycle in 2024. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) enables you as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions. 


The projects will be open for customer registration until July 12, 2024. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public and Private Edition projects for registration: 

 

AI assistant for General Ledger Accountant

AI-based warehouse order streaming, task optimization and resource planning

Availability Check in Two-Tier ERP Subsidiary Business

Basic End-to-end make-to-order / assemble-to-order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

GRC 2026 for SAP GRC Solutions Private Cloud Edition / On-Premise  

Intelligent assistant capabilities for Project Systems in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Lockout Tagout / Isolation Management in Permit to Work

Risk and Assurance Reporting supported by AI

SAP Project and Resource Management – Scheduling of projects

Taulia receivable financing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register. 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP. 

Check out the full list, if you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

entry.title: Register Now- SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Partner Certification Academies, Q2, 2024 Edition.
entry.content:
Register now to the upcoming Schedule of Academies:

  1. SAP Partner Academy - Implementation Consultant  – 25th June- Register (Mandatory for all LOBs)
  2. SAP Partner Academy - Financial Accounting  – 26th June-  Register
  3. SAP Partner Academy - Sales  – 27th June- Register
  4. SAP Partner Academy - Sourcing & Procurement  – 01st July-   Register

What is the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Partner Certification Academy?

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Partner Certification Academies are expert led virtual sessions, designed to provide Certification-exam-preparation guidance for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editions Role based Certifications for Implementation Consultant, Financial Accounting, Sales and Souring & Procurement.

  • Learn More here

How do the academies work?

The academies, delivered by SAP Certified experts, provide guidance and help learners to build familiarity & certification guidance across the various topics, covered in the SAP Learning Journeys.  

What is the goal of the academies?

To help learners, prepare and successfully pass for their Certification exam!

What are the benefits of joining the academies?

  • The academies help to optimize the time, spent by a learner, in the self-paced consumption of LJs.
  • A high-level overview of key concepts, tips & tricks to optimally consume the SAP Learning Journeys, to prepare for a Certification Exam.
  • Help learners, devise an optimal learning plan to tackle their certification attempt.
  • Enhances and accelerates the learning path in effectively progressing through an SAP Learning Journey to prepare for an SAP Certification Exam.

What are the Certifications covered?

Topic

Name of Certification

Role

Code

Implementation Fundamentals

SAP Certified Associate -Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition –

·       Implementation Consultant

·       Also recommended for Project Managers

(C_S4CPB)

Finance

SAP Certified Associate – Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition -Financial Accounting

Implementation Consultant

(C_S4CFI)

Sales

Implementation Consultant

(C_S4CS).

Sourcing & Procurement

SAP Certified Associate – Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sourcing & Procurement

Implementation Consultant

(C_S4CPR)

 

Who is it meant for?

All aspiring LOB Implementation consultants and project managers, that are looking to get certified on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Role Based & LoB Certifications.

Is there any fee?

This is a free of charge offering. However, it is mandatory that the Partner has access to a full subscription of SAP Learning Hub, to access all the content of the SAP Learning Journeys for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Self-study of the Learning Journeys is mandatory, after attending the academy and before appearing for the Certification exam.

More Questions:

Please write to sapna.subramaniam@sap.com in case of more questions!

entry.title: Ever Wondered about the Psychology behind Transformation?
entry.content:
Many articles start with the quote “Change is the only constant”. Having moved continents and stepped into my 19th house, I would say change has been on multivitamin pills in my life.

Every time we get out of the comfort zone and rake up the dormant normalcy, be it in our personal or professional lives, we come face to face with reality and the need to make decisions that matter. What adds value and what should I carry forward with me? How am I going to do it? These are the ripples on the layer and when you dig deeper the question one might have to ask to get to the root of it all is - Why am I doing this and why do I need to hold onto something?

If we as individuals need so much of pondering and courage to take the next step, imagine what it takes for businesses to recognize and lead change.

Did I just say recognize? I would say it again with a bold emphasis. Asking the ‘why’ 3 times can get you to a different answer. Let’s take an example.

Why do you have this process?

Answer 1: Because it has been in place for the last 30 years.

Why do you have this process?

Answer 2: Because everyone in the organization is used to it and we continue to do that and believe (or assume) everything is working fine so far.

Why do you have this process?

Answer 3: Hmm, pause…we don’t know why we do what we do as this was laid down 30 years ago and we haven't had the time or the thought to look into it.

The logical next question is why are we not changing a process for better relevance and efficiency? A business needs a visionary power to recognize that there is a need for change and courage to help everyone embrace the transformation with a clear understanding of the 'Why' behind it. Transformation is a continuous process and it is an era of 'Change Leadership'.

A sentence “let go and rejoice” from a book on Indian philosophy stared at me as I was packing the carton of books.

With an exclamation and a question mark on my mind, I was wondering how rejoicing can become a logical consequence of letting go what we possess? My thoughts drifted to the amazing concept of subtractive transformation.

The researchers from University of Virginia conducted an experiment to improve a Lego structure which was out of balance. Majority of the participants took to adding a Lego brick rather than removing one which could have been the easiest way to stabilize and improve the structure.

This boils down to the question of why we as humans attribute addition as a synonym to improvement and shy away from subtracting something to improve the condition. This can be applied to every facet of our lives- minimalism, sustainable operations, fast fashion, clogged meeting schedules, and the list is endless. Imagine the processes and long-standing customizations in a company and how a subtractive transformation can help add efficiency and cumulatively enhance the experience of the people and the planet.

We are the most complex of beings and it takes great might and vision to recognize and lead change.

As I write one more sentence, I look at that one empty wall in my new home wondering if I must improve it by adding a work of art that I recently saw in a store that read “less is more” … 😊

entry.title: Customer Engagement Initiative: Taulia Receivables Financing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
According to OECD, innovative and growth oriented firms, as well as medium-sized enterprises seeking to invest and expand and SME seeking to undertake green investments are facing capital gaps in form of lack of access to liquidity, higher interest rates and tighter borrowing terms.

Help me tackling this! Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative focusing on Taulia Receivables Financing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition! 

By turning receivables into cash, you can improve your financial KPI and fuel growth, even in today's challenging economic environment.

With Taulia's cutting-edge technology and multi-funder infrastructure integrated into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we're enabling quicker access to funding through the Taulia Funding Platform, and automation of transactions, journal posting, and reconciliation.

Join us for a 1:1 discovery session to share your insights and shape the future of receivables finance on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Registration is open until July, 11th!

Together, we'll gather requirements and use cases, identify pain points, validate design and concept, and prioritize features and processes to ensure maximum value for your finance function.

 

🌍Regions: APJ, EMEA, Americas

🏢Company Size: Mid-size Enterprise, Large Enterprises

🎯Targeted User Profile: CFO, Head of Finance, Treasury Manager, IT Business Process Owner Finance with experience in receivables financing

 

Partners are explicitly invited! 

 

Don't miss out on this opportunity to make a difference in your finance operations! Register now and let's shape the future together!

entry.title: Standard Scope vs Public Sector Scope: A Key Decision for SAP Public Sector Implementations
entry.content:
Whether it's your calling or career, as an implementation consultant, you’ll frequently find yourself untangling the complex web of ERP systems. 
This blog provides a deeper dive into the Standard Scope vs Public Sector Scope debate, to help you navigate the complex web of an ERP system within the Public Sector.


1. Key Choices

Understanding when to use Public Sector Management (PSM) instead of the Enterprise Management (EM) scope. The answer lies in identifying whether there are elements within a project that correspond with various facets associated with the public sector. For instance, if you’re dealing with multilateral reporting across different account assignments, consider the Public Sector Scope. This scope is also essential for year-end processes tied to government frameworks or specific governmental budgetary processes.
Below we have summarized and illustrated the key choices you have to make to decide on the correct Scope Version:


2. Public Sector Management Considerations 

Public Sector Management implementation also captures specific to fund accounting. This is instrumental for nonprofit governmental entities, especially in North America, that need to maintain checks and balances on the usage and distribution of resources. For instance, the reporting framework within PSM is multifaceted. It allows for data to be grouped, controlled, and analyzed across multiple dimensions, such as fund, grant, budget period, budget account, functional area, organization, or project. Additionally, other processes such as commitment carry-forward and residual budget processing, specific to government-related year-end procedures, are also part of PSM functionality.

Moreover, government-related budgetary processes need a unique approach. For instance, increasing the budget from revenue is a requirement for many government agencies. To enable proficient control and oversee these processes effectively, Public Sector Management is equipped with these functionalities, thereby eliminating obstacles and facilitating these specialized processes.

Furthermore, when dealing with grants, within PSM, SAP delivers special processes such as indirect cost and cost-sharing. In a public sector organization dealing with myriad grants, these processes become vital, impacting budgeting and resource allocation. Similarly, the PSM Scope utilizes period-based commitment updating to ensure the precise tracking of all commitments using accounting practices.

In regards of budget consumption and asset acquisition, the PSM Scope recognizes expenses, payments, and collections instead of costs and revenues. By configuring your system with the PSM  Scope, this functionality transitions the system from merely tracking depreciation to understanding and recording the consumption of budget during asset acquisition.

When managing costs and expenses, the PSM Scope offers a detailed perspective, differentiating between the two. In the corporate world, an expense usually equates to cost. However, in the public sector, an expense should be differentiated as a commitment that leads to future payment, while cost refers to the process that doesn't always result in a future money outflow (for example, depreciation). This differentiation provides a more detailed report, enabling better financial management within public sector organizations.

In terms of integrations, the PSM Scope is already integrated with SAP SuccessFactors for position budget control and with Concur for commitments. These integrations not only enhance the system’s capabilities but also simplify the overall operations of a public sector organization.


3. Evaluation 

No doubt using the PSM Scope comes with its own set of limitations. One limit to note when implementing the PSM Scope is the automatic activation of the baseline scope, which includes around 180 choosable scope items. These items are defined based on the typical needs of public sector organizations. Nonetheless, some scopes can appear to be a hard boundary for public sector needs, such as sales incentives, cost of sales, and so forth. It’s important to note that SAP will continue to further improve the Standard Scope items in the future based on market needs. 

Considering these complexities, it's recommended that consultants carefully evaluate their clients’ needs and evaluate whether the use of Public Sector Management is needed and beneficial. The takeaway is that a one-size-fits-all approach won’t suffice in understanding these scopes. Careful assessment and tailored solutions will be the key to unlocking the real potential of your ERP implementations for the public sector. In addition, partners may find the vast number of available scope items overwhelming, raising questions about which choices best correspond to the organization’s needs.



4. Conclusion

It’s essential to understand that each government organization will have unique requirements. For example, we had customers in certain countries/regions who categorized taxpayer accounting as part of ERP. Other government departments might have different definitions of ERP and accounting processes, which can involve the calculation and collection of taxes. In conclusion, it's important to remember that every institution will have unique requirements, and while the PSM Scope offers a wide range of functionalities, consultants should always prioritize their clients' needs. Applying a flexible approach, coupled with an in-depth understanding of the standard and public sector scopes, will truly enable consultants to extract the highest performance from their ERP implementations, ultimately benefitting the organization at large. Therefore, the Standard Scope vs Public Sector Scope isn’t a competition but rather a chance to maximize functionality based on each entity's special needs.


Co-Authored w/ Frank Godeby, Public Sector Product Management

entry.title: Discover the World of Poultry: From Farm to Fork with E2E Supply chain in SAP S4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Implementing SAP S4HANA by adopting baseline best practices to build solution with minor customizations in the poultry industry involves managing the entire supply chain and logistic from purchasing of raw materials, vaccines and medicines etc. production of different feeds based on requirement of broilers and breeders, procuring or producing eggs from breeders, producing chicks from egg, growing chicks to chicken and slaughtering of chicken to produce poultry products to consumers. Using SAP S4HANA, this can be effectively managed through various integrated modules namely MM, FI, CO, PP, QM, SD, PS and SAC. Here’s a comprehensive approach to achieving a proper solution in poultry using SAP S4HANA capabilities. The SAP baseline best practices approach ensures processes required to deploy by scope items and can be implemented which cover the entire poultry supply chain with financial. Below holistic view in Verticals of Poultry industry will give us crisp understanding.

Verticals of Poultry are:

Feed Mill:

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure and maintaining inventory of corn, soya, vitamins, minerals, produced finished feeds. SAP production planning (PP) for formulations as recipes, consume raw materials and produce feeds with batch. SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for Feed. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell Feeds to out growers, customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Rearing and Breeding (Parent birds):

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure parent birds and maintaining inventory of feeds, medicines, vitamins, minerals, disinfectants, produced hatchable eggs, SAP production planning (PP) for rearing birds & laying birds with recording of culls and mortality. consume feed, medicines, vitamins, minerals and producing hatchable eggs with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for rearing and growing. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell hatchable eggs and manure to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Hatchery:

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure hatchable eggs and maintaining inventory of vaccines, medicines, vitamins, minerals, disinfectants, produced chicks (DOC), SAP production planning (PP) for sorting, setting, hatching with recording of culls and mortality. consume vaccines, medicines and producing Chicks (DOC) with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for Hatching. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell DOC and Unhatched eggs to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Broiler:

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure chicks (DOC) and maintaining inventory of vaccines, medicines, vitamins, minerals, disinfectants, produced broilers (live bird), SAP production planning (PP) for growing birds with recording of culls and mortality. consume vaccines, medicines and producing broilers (live bird) with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for broilers. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell broilers (live bird) and manure to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Processing and Packaging Plant (PPL):

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure broilers (live bird) and maintaining inventory of spices, packing, disinfectants, produced products, SAP production planning (PP) for slaughtering, grading, cutting, deboning etc and consume spices, packing and producing finished products with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for poultry products. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell poultry products to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability. Manage stock transfers from Processing plant to DCs with SAP Material Management (MM) and SAP Sales and distribution (SD).

Distribution centers to Sales outlets or retail outlets:

Van sales, wholesale, Retail customer outlets with SAP Sales and Distribution (SD) to Manage sales orders and distribution sell poultry products to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Implementation Steps

Assessment and Planning: Conduct a thorough assessment of current organisation structure and define the scope items to be used.

System Design: Design the SAP solution architecture with baseline best practices and customize to the specific needs of the poultry supply chain.

Customization and Configuration: Customize SAP modules to fit the unique requirements of the poultry industry with catch weight management, PPDS, aATP, Segmentation etc.

Integration: Integrate SAP with existing systems, IoT devices, and third-party applications.

Data Migration: Migrate existing data into the SAP system, ensuring data integrity and completeness with LTMC.

Training and Change Management: Provide training to users and manage the transition to the new system with enable now.

Go-Live and Continuous Improvement: Launch the system and continuously monitor and improve processes based on feedback and data insights.

Benefits

Revenue on investment: Investing on SAP S4HANA private cloud will turn your investment cost to Operating Cost where no need to invest on hardware and baseline best practices provides more functionalities with less maintenance cost rather Investing in multiple software systems

Single source of truth: Streamlined operations with respect to processes can be run in S4HANA without multiple systems (third party).

Better Decision-Making: Data-driven insights can be made available from single source for strategic planning and operational improvements.

Mobile Applications: Deploy SAP Fiori apps for mobile, tablet, PC to access critical data and operations to manage on-the-go.

Conclusion

Using SAP S4HANA private Cloud in the poultry industry ensures a comprehensive, integrated approach to managing the entire supply chain. By leveraging SAP’s robust ERP capabilities and advanced technologies the poultry businesses can achieve greater efficiency, traceability, and profitability. Successful implementation requires careful planning to utilize baseline best practices with customization and continuous improvement to adapt to changing market demands and regulatory requirements.

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance - Roadmap Session at SAP Sapphire
entry.content:
(If you prefer listening to reading, you can also watch this video where I cover the content of this post in an online session.)

I recently outlined our plans for the SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) solution portfolio at Sapphire, Orlando.

The MDG roadmap session was fully packed at Sapphire and our plans resonated well with the audience. In fact, at the beginning of the session there was still a big crowd of people standing outside the room waiting for entry, but as the room was already completely full they could unfortunately not attend for security reasons.

I would like to apologize for the fact that some of you were not let in. And to make up for this at least a bit, I'd like to share the news with you in the SAP Community. (The slides below are actually the ones that I used in that Sapphire roadmap session.)

Please note that the roadmap disclaimer indicating that future items are subject to change applies.

Solution Introduction

To get started, let me very briefly explain what SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) is, and which topics it addresses.

 

 

MDG is an application to manage master data, it provides preconfigured, domain-specific master data governance to monitor, find and correct data quality issues. You can either centrally create, change, and distribute, or you can consolidate master data coming from different systems in your complete enterprise landscape.

SAP MDG is one application for all master data. It is not only for SAP master data. It does come with a lot of out-of-the-box, domain-specific capabilities that are, of course, based on SAP’s data models and business logic. But it is also a platform that is used by many customers to build master data governance processes on extended data models and completely custom-defined master data domains.

From our customers, we understand that the purpose of master data management is really to improve the quality of master data and to get a corporate-wide understanding of master data, so that they can run and optimize their business on top of it.

As you might know, companies are taking different approaches to MDM. For example, some allow for de-central ownership of master data, and create a common understanding by using master data consolidation to create key mapping between detected duplicates and to create a golden record. Others prefer central ownership of master data and distribute master data that is always created in high quality. Some companies decide to take a de-central approach for some domains – like customer master data, for example – and a central approach for other domains – like when introducing new products, for example. And on top of that, many companies constantly monitor how the quality of master data evolves. And then they react, if they find issues.

SAP MDG supports all these approaches and also all combinations of these approaches in a very integrated and organized way, and this is very much appreciated by our customers. This allows you to combine approaches as required to cover all use cases and all implementation styles, for a complete enterprise master data management program with just one solution: SAP MDG.

Strategy and Vision

Our strategy and vision for MDG is very consistent over the last years. And we are very successfully executing along this vision. And you will also see that it is not an isolated strategy just for MDG. Our MDG product strategy is embedded into SAP’s broader business solutions strategy as well as the SAP BTP strategy.

Our roadmap for MDG is driven by – our core beliefs – and by trends that we see in the market. We believe, SAP can provide much more value to our customers than just any MDM tool provider, because we understand how master data is used in business context. We create benefits for our customers leveraging this understanding. We can provide master data management capabilities that truly support all domains, all industries, and all approaches, embedded into the business. We have proven this with SAP MDG, and we will continue to deliver on this unique promise.

In addition, we see certain trends in the market. And I will talk about these trends in more detail on the next slides. On this slide, I just want to highlight how interrelated these trends are. 

For example, new sources for trusted data in the cloud, or data sharing in business networks in the cloud, will allow for more automation in keeping your master data records up to date.

Or take the fact that Digitization demands for higher volumes and better quality of master data, and hence needs more automation in master data management.

As we all know, the market for business application software is moving towards modular applications and towards the cloud. For most companies, this move to the cloud is not one single step. And to allow for a step-by-step journey, modularization is a key enabler, that allows to move also MDM capabilities to the cloud step-by-step, as required by the applications.

Let’s come to the first trend: Cloud. When business applications are moving to the cloud, the master data itself needs to be provided there. And this is of course possible with MDG today. But in addition, we will continue to provide cloud-based master data integration services on the SAP Business Technology Platform. And we will build out data quality services as microservices in the cloud. Like today we are offering Address-Validation-as-a-Service against always up-to-date address directories, in the cloud.

I already mentioned that a stepwise move of business applications to the cloud demands for a modular approach to MDM. We are addressing this need with federation of MDG. This will by the way also help to meet the increasingly local compliance regulations.

Modularization and cloud consumption will also enable our customers to get to a positive return on investment much faster and to grow their enterprise MDM program exactly in the pace needed for their strategic business initiatives. 

I think we all agree that master data is the key resource of any digital transformation. We will enhance MDG to help companies to become more intelligent, more agile, and to always process data compliantly.

Digitization will demand for modular, specialized business applications and we will help to provide harmonized, high-quality master data to these applications to enable end-to-end processes for Intelligent Enterprises. The key drivers that we are focusing on in MDG are automation enablement, efficiency, and master data quality management.

In the modular application landscape of the future, it will be a challenge to provide frictionless digital processes. SAP’s Business Technology Platform will be the foundation to achieve this. SAP One Domain Model as the common language and SAP Master Data Integration for SAP and non-SAP integration will enable this.

And this will provide our customers with a very high return on their investment into master data quality, because BTP’s common data foundation will allow to then broadly apply artificial intelligence as well as analytical scenarios to all data, and this is then powered by high quality master data provided by SAP MDG. This means that every investment that you put into improved master data with MDG, will pay back significantly in all your data-driven scenarios on top of BTP.

We actually also see clear proof of this interrelation of digitization and investments into master data management, as many of the current SAP S/4HANA transformation programs at our customers are using SAP MDG to prepare this fundamental move for their company.

As discussed, digitization is one of the key drivers for automation in MDM. We will leverage SAP’s deep knowledge in business applications, analytics, and business networks, to reduce the need for manual tasks in achieving high master data quality.

We are leveraging third-party data providers to drive automation in master data creation. We are providing ways to predict master data attribute values from application context, and we plan to continue to use more artificial intelligence to support master data quality tasks. This will help to reduce the human effort in MDM and will help to avoid errors created by humans, and to avoid the effort it takes to correct these human errors.

This kind of automation will need an agile, rapidly-scaling MDM solution, that can be extended fast, scales quickly, and easily integrates with new systems, new business processes and new data types. We think, on the long run a modular approach to MDM will be more effective to meet this agility, more effective than a monolithic approach.

Let’s now focus on Federation. I have pointed out earlier how a more modular application landscape requires a modular approach to MDM and what other benefits in agility, speed, efficiency, and TCO we expect from that approach. Therefore, we provide this additional deployment option for MDG.

This can be an option for companies that plan to balance central and decentral MDG processes instead of using a fully centrally managed MDG approach.

This can for example be an alternative for very heterogeneous companies running very differently set up business processes and application systems in their landscape, and for example also multiple differently configured SAP S/4HANA systems.

We believe in providing choices and non-disruptive journeys for our customers. That is why this additional deployment option is not only for new customers but can also be used by existing customers by adding to their current implementation and safeguarding their investments into SAP MDG.

As of today, most companies are using one single MDG system to centrally-manage the master data for the complete enterprise. And everybody can of course continue to do so. But for some companies, one single monolithic MDM system for the complete company already today provides some challenges. And as we discussed, this will even increase in the future.

The new approach with a federated MDG landscape will allow these companies to go for a more modular approach. They can for example get their MDM journey started at very low initial investment by just subscribing to a highly standardized Software-as-a-Service solution just for core master data attributes. And then in the future they can grow into a platform approach. Or they can federate out to more application-specific deployments of MDG.

We believe that we will provide companies with a faster time to value, with increased flexibility that enables a broader MDM scope, and with a lower Total-Cost-of-Ownership.

Federation allows companies to execute master data management as central as possible, but also as local as needed by the diverse parts of the organization. They can really govern the data at the place where it is best understood. For more details, you may want to read the blog post about the benefits of federation.

SAP offers choices how to consume SAP Master Data Governance across the cloud and on premise. Companies can hence decide for their deployment approach of choice.

On the left-hand side, you see SAP MDG on SAP S/4HANA, private cloud edition – which can also be deployed on premise. This version is typically used for multi-domain, centralized governance across complete enterprises. It has pre-delivered business content across various domains (for example, business partner, customer, supplier, material, financial data), and there are solution extensions from SAP Partners. This version comes with a very flexible framework to extend processes or create custom master data objects that even meet the very complex needs of the largest enterprises.

In the middle, there is SAP MDG on the public cloud version of SAP S/4HANA. This is typically for organizations who are focused on consumption in the public cloud, and who are running SAP S/4HANA in the public cloud. This version is currently focuses on business partner, customer, supplier, and product data. It is easy to consume and provides the processes for the very broad underlying S/4HANA data model.

And then, SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition – on the right-hand side – is a software-as-a-service kick-start solution for the governance of core Business Partner data.

We will continue to enhance all three versions of MDG. As you can read on the bottom of the page, the strategy is that all versions of MDG will have the same broad coverage of master data management processes across central governance, consolidation, data quality management and so on.
